DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT EBAY, INC.'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AS TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR DOCUMENT PRODUCTION, SET TWO, REQUEST NO. 7; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS - MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO DESIGNATE MATERIALS AS "CONFIDENTIAL." COURT FINDS THAT POTENTIAL DISCLOSURE OF TRADE SECRETS IS ADEQUATE BASIS FOR ISSUANCE OF PROTECTIVE ORDER, AND NO COUNTERVAILING PUBLIC INTEREST IN DISCLOSURE HAS BEEN DEMONSTRATED. BASED ON REPRESENTATION THAT THE CURRENT FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IDENTIFIED THE NECESSITY TO SEEK AN ORDER NOW, RATHER THAN EARLIER, THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THE REQUEST SO UNTIMELY AS TO CONSTITUTE A WAIVER. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS BY BOTH PARTIES IS DENIED. (A) AS TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST, COURT FINDS THAT THE REQUEST FOR THE PROTECTIVE ORDER SHOULD HAVE BEEN MADE ON OR BEFORE THE RESPONSE TO THE ORIGINAL MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES. HOWEVER, THE COURT HAS DISCRETION TO ORDER A PROTECTIVE ORDER AT THIS TIME UNDER C.C.P. 2031.060 AND SO GRANTS THE MOTION. (B) AS TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST, THE COURT FINDS GOOD CAUSE TO ISSUE THE PROTECTIVE ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: NILS ROSENQUEST; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-19-2014 Continued To Apr-01-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order As To Pltf'S Request For Document Production, Set Two, Request No. 7; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michelle C. Kirrane, Esq. of LeClairRyan LLP, Tel. 415.913.4917, for defendant and moving party;
Joseph S. May, Esq. of the Law Office of Joseph S. May, Tel. 415.781.3333, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT EBAY, INC.'s Motion For Protective Order As To Plaintiff'S Request For Document Production, Set Two, Request No. 7; Request For Sanctions.
Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Motion is granted for protective order to designate materials as "confidential." Court finds that potential disclosure of trade secrets is adequate basis for issuance of protective order, and no countervailing public interest in disclosure has been demonstrated. Based on representation that the current further production of documents identified the necessity to seek an order now, rather than earlier, the Court does not find the request so untimely as to constitute a waiver.
Request for sanctions by both parties is denied.
(a) As to defendant's request, Court finds that the request for the protective order should have been made on or before the response to the original motion to compel further responses. However, the Court has discretion to order a protective order at this time under C.C.P. 2031.060 and so grants the motion.
(b) As to plaintiff's request, the Court finds good cause to issue the protective order.
Prevailing party to submit a proposed order. Judge Pro Tem: Nils Rosenquest; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
